behaviors.description: Exponential backoff: wait time doubles with each retry, capped at configured maximum
behaviors.justification: Pure calculation logic. Mock delay function verifies correct durations (1x, 2x, 4x, …, max) passed from library. Real time or async semantics not needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: mock delay function, call library with increasing retry counts, assert durations follow exponential sequence 1x → 2x → 4x → … → max. No actual time passage required.

behaviors.description: Library correctly identifies rate-limit response (429) and computes wait time from headers or default
behaviors.justification: Response parsing logic. Mock HTTP responses with various Retry-After formats verify the library correctly extracts or computes wait duration. No real network needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: mock HTTP responses with 429 status and Retry-After headers (seconds, HTTP-date, missing); verify library correctly parses header or applies default backoff. Test edge cases (missing header, malformed date).

behaviors.description: After backoff delay, library retries original request with same parameters and transparently returns successful result
behaviors.justification: Retry behavior testable with mock: return 429 first, then 200. Verify call count=2, params unchanged, result returned. Mock delay function verifies intent to wait. No real async semantics needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: mock HTTP client returns 429 on first call, 200 on second. Call library method. Assert: (1) two HTTP calls made with identical request parameters, (2) successful 200 response returned transparently to caller, (3) delay function called with correct duration before retry.

behaviors.description: After max retries exhausted, return typed QuotaExhaustedError (not generic error)
behaviors.justification: Retry counting and error type are local logic. Mock HTTP always returning 429 lets us verify exact retry count and error type. No real network needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: mock HTTP to always return 429. Call library. Assert: (1) QuotaExhaustedError returned (not generic HTTPError or similar), (2) exactly N retries attempted (call count = N+1 including initial), (3) error message is appropriate, (4) no retry beyond max.

behaviors.description: Full retry chain terminates correctly; does not loop indefinitely before surfacing error
behaviors.justification: Termination testable with mocks + test timeout. Infinite loop caught by timeout hanging. Mock HTTP returning 429 indefinitely, set timeout; if code loops forever, test times out and fails.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: mock HTTP to always return 429. Set test timeout (e.g., 5 seconds). Call library. Assert: (1) test completes within timeout (infinite loop → timeout failure), (2) QuotaExhaustedError returned, (3) no hang or deadlock. Timeout itself is the failure detector.

behaviors.description: Multiple concurrent async callers in same process share same quota bucket; all requests draw from single quota
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Mock HTTP alone cannot verify shared quota semantics across concurrent calls without real async operations. Race conditions and quota isolation bugs only surface under actual concurrent load. Must test multiple concurrent async calls through library.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: launch M concurrent async calls to library.call(). Mock HTTP tracks shared quota budget (e.g., decrement on each call, return 429 after K calls). Assert: (1) all concurrent calls draw from same quota bucket, (2) quota depletes consistently (no per-request isolation), (3) quota updates are atomic (no corruption under concurrent access), (4) quota state consistent across all concurrent callers.

behaviors.description: Multiple waiting requests dispatched in FIFO order (order submitted = order dispatched)
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: FIFO queue logic alone is Unit-testable, but queue ordering under concurrent async load requires real concurrent operations. Race conditions and dispatch reordering bugs surface only under actual concurrent stress.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: submit M concurrent async calls to library while quota exhausted (forcing queue). Track submission timestamps/IDs and dispatch order. Assert: (1) dispatch order strictly matches submission order (FIFO), (2) no reordering under concurrent load, (3) queue fairness maintained, (4) each request waits its turn but does not jump queue.
